ai_strategy_lead.name: Jenny Leonard
ai_strategy_lead.title: Associate Vice President of Digital Strategy (AI Council, Administrative domain co-lead)

documents.title: Artificial Intelligence at the University of Rochester (AI Council portal)
Boundless Possibility — 2030 Strategic Plan (AI woven across research, education, health, creative mission)
Responsible Use of AI Tools and Institutional Data Security (Office of the Provost)

ai_strategy.summary: Rochester frames AI as a cross-cutting enabler inside its 2030 Boundless Possibility strategic plan and operationalizes that framing through a President/Provost-sponsored AI Council organized into five domains (Administrative, AI Tools, Education, Clinical, Research). The university has stood up a secure ChatGPT-based platform (chat.rochester.edu / UR AI Chat v2 on Azure OpenAI), issued multiple policy documents in 2024–2025, and joined New York's Empire AI consortium as one of only two newly admitted R1 partners. A proposed AI Horizons Institute (led by CS professor Christopher Kanan) is a centerpiece of the research strategy.

key_provisions: Instructors must publish a syllabus-level GenAI policy specifying when students must, may, or cannot use GenAI on each assignment
Non-public or sensitive University data may not be uploaded to public AI tools; only chat.rochester.edu is approved for moderate- and high-risk data
Researchers must restrict GenAI inputs to low-risk data and comply with HIPAA, FERPA, and IP policies
GenAI use must be disclosed in research proposals, publications, dissertations, and capstone projects, with advance committee approval for theses
GenAI detection tools are characterized as unreliable and biased; human oversight is required for grading and feedback
Policy references NIST AI 600-1 and UNESCO AI-in-education guidance
ai_policy.summary: The University of Rochester adopted a comprehensive generative-AI policy framework between December 2024 and May 2025, layering an Education guidance (Provost), a Research guidance (AI in Research Committee), and an institutional data-security guidance. The stance is permissive-with-structure: faculty retain academic freedom but must post syllabus-level rules, and every constituent is bound by data-classification limits tied to the university's internal chat.rochester.edu platform.

bodies.name: University AI Council
bodies.scope: University-wide body organizing AI governance into five domain tracks: Administrative, AI Tools, Education, Clinical (URMC), and Research. Co-leads include Jenny Leonard, Courtney Peplin, Robert Evangelista, James Forrester, Edwin van Wijngaarden, Lisa Nelson, and AnnMarie White.

bodies.name: AI in Research Committee
bodies.scope: Sub-committee of the Faculty Senate chartered by the Research Executive Committee; publishes and maintains Responsible Use of GenAI in Research guidelines.

bodies.name: GenAI in Education Working Group
bodies.scope: Education-domain workstream under the AI Council; led by Assistant Vice Provost Edwin van Wijngaarden; produces and revises the Gen AI Use in Education Guidance.

bodies.name: University Marketing and Communications Generative AI Committee
bodies.scope: Develops GenAI guidelines specific to marketing, web, and brand communications use.

bodies.name: URMC Clinical AI governance (led through the Chief Applications Officer)
bodies.scope: Clinical-domain AI review and deployment governance for UR Medicine; stood up alongside Qualified Health and Dragon Copilot rollouts.

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed across an AI Council with five domain tracks, each co-led by a named administrator, plus a Faculty Senate AI in Research Committee and targeted sub-committees for education, marketing, and clinical AI. There is no single Chief AI Officer; accountability runs through the Provost, VP for Research, VP for IT/CIO, and URMC CAO.

initiatives.title: Launch and expansion of chat.rochester.edu (UR AI Chat v2 on Azure OpenAI / GPT-4o)
initiatives.description: Rochester launched a university-licensed ChatGPT instance at chat.rochester.edu in January 2025 and released v2 on May 9, 2025, moving to GPT-4o on Azure OpenAI and adding a Document Agent for PDF/Word/text uploads. The platform is approved for moderate- and high-risk institutional data and is accessible to all UR and URMC users via SSO and Duo.
initiatives.significance: Gives Rochester one of the earliest campus-wide secure GenAI platforms among mid-size private R1s, and is the anchor that lets the Provost restrict sensitive data from public tools.

investment.funding_source: Central IT, Office of the Provost
investment.description: License fees and Azure consumption for campus-wide GPT-4o access; dollar amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: GPT-4o delivered through Microsoft's Azure OpenAI tenant; OpenAI licensing routed via Microsoft partnership.

tools_and_vendors.name: UR AI Chat v2 (chat.rochester.edu)
tools_and_vendors.role: Secure ChatGPT-style assistant with Document Agent

initiatives.policy_implications: Pairs with the Provost's March 2025 data-security directive that bars sensitive data from public AI tools; the internal platform is the sanctioned alternative.

initiatives.title: University of Rochester joins Empire AI consortium
initiatives.description: UR (and RIT and Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai) were admitted to Empire AI in February 2025, joining founding members SUNY, CUNY, Columbia, Cornell, NYU, RPI, and the Flatiron Institute. New York State's FY26 budget added $90M capital and $25M/10-year SUNY operating funding on top of the original $275M commitment; $50M in private funding is tied to the newer members.
initiatives.significance: Gives Rochester shared access to the state-funded AI computing center at the University of Buffalo and embeds UR as the only consortium member with an integrated health system (eight hospitals) for clinical AI research.

investment.funding_source: New York State (Empire AI); additional $90M capital + $25M/10yr operating; $50M private
investment.description: State-led consortium investment of $275M initial plus $90M capital and $25M/10yr operating in FY26, with $50M in new private funding. Rochester's specific share is not disclosed.

partners: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
collaborations.description: Statewide research compute and collaboration consortium anchored at University at Buffalo.
Primary public funder of Empire AI's compute center and operating budget.

initiatives.policy_implications: Binds UR to shared state AI compute governance and aligns its research with NY's public-interest AI priorities.

initiatives.title: URMC enterprise-wide deployment of Qualified Health GenAI platform
initiatives.description: On October 1, 2025, URMC announced it will license and deploy Qualified Health's enterprise-wide generative AI platform across approximately 32,000 employees (including 3,000+ researchers and 2,000 physicians) spanning eight hospitals, an NCI-designated cancer center, and the Schools of Medicine, Dentistry, and Nursing. The platform provides HIPAA-compliant chat, tailored AI agents, governance controls, and real-time monitoring to automate administrative and regulatory workflows.
initiatives.significance: One of the largest single-contract enterprise GenAI rollouts in academic medicine to date, and the operational backbone of URMC's clinical AI strategy.

investment.description: Multi-year enterprise contract; specific dollar amount not disclosed.

tools_and_vendors.name: Qualified Health platform
tools_and_vendors.role: HIPAA-compliant enterprise GenAI platform with agents and governance controls

initiatives.policy_implications: Deployed with explicit guardrails and governance controls; framed by CMIO Gregg Nicandri as 'right guardrails' being prerequisite to positive AI transformation.

initiatives.title: URMC PowerScribe One + Microsoft Dragon Copilot in radiology
initiatives.description: URMC migrated to cloud-based PowerScribe One and is in preview with Microsoft Dragon Copilot, using Smart Impression (generative AI auto-drafting of radiology impressions in each radiologist's style) and prior-report summarization for imaging workflows. Led operationally by Stacie Barnard (Director, Integrated Clinical Systems) and Dr. Sean Cleary (Vice Chair of Informatics for Imaging Sciences).
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates URMC's early adoption of named-vendor clinical GenAI inside a top-tier imaging workflow, and anchors Microsoft's case studies for Dragon Copilot in academic medicine.

investment.description: Subscription and licensing through Microsoft; dollar amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: PowerScribe One (Nuance, now Microsoft) and Dragon Copilot deployed in URMC radiology.

tools_and_vendors.name: PowerScribe One
tools_and_vendors.role: Cloud-based radiology speech recognition and impression auto-draft

tools_and_vendors.name: Dragon Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Generative AI companion for ambient clinical documentation and prior-report summarization

initiatives.policy_implications: Deployed under URMC clinical AI governance with preview-phase guardrails on Dragon Copilot.

initiatives.title: AI Horizons Institute — proposed NSF AI Institute
initiatives.description: A transdisciplinary AI Horizons Institute, led by Associate Professor Christopher Kanan (CS, Center for Visual Science) with co-leads spanning education, philosophy, computational linguistics, psychology, and medicine, submitted an NSF AI Institute proposal during a one-year planning effort funded through the Provost's transdisciplinary RFP. The institute focuses on generative and agentic AI informed by neuroscience, psychology, and philosophy, with a healthcare application arm.
initiatives.significance: Represents Rochester's bid to move from a mid-sized AI research footprint (fewer than ten core AI faculty) toward a federally funded AI institute peer to Cornell, Columbia, and NYU.

investment.funding_source: Provost's transdisciplinary RFP planning award; proposed NSF AI Institute
investment.description: One of ten 2024–2025 planning awards drawn from Mangelsdorf's $8.5M transdisciplinary research center commitment; NSF AI Institute award not yet decided.

initiatives.policy_implications: Design includes explicit ethical, educational, and healthcare application arms, reflecting UR's cautious-with-ethics stance.

initiatives.title: GenAI in Education Guidance (syllabus-level policy + faculty guidance)
initiatives.description: Adopted December 2, 2024 and revised in March and May 2025, the Office of the Provost's GenAI in Education Guidance requires every instructor to publish a syllabus-level policy specifying when students must, may, or cannot use GenAI on each assignment. The document codifies principles covering academic integrity, bias, privacy, transparency, accountability, equitable access, and academic freedom.
initiatives.significance: Places Rochester among the R1 private universities with an enforceable syllabus-level requirement rather than just advisory guidance.

investment.description: Internal policy development; no dedicated dollar amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires syllabus-level GenAI rules across the university; references NIST AI 600-1 and UNESCO guidance.

initiatives.title: Responsible Use of GenAI in Research guidance
initiatives.description: Published December 13, 2024 by the Faculty Senate AI in Research Committee, the guidance bounds research use of GenAI by three principles: data protection (only low-risk data into web-based tools), verification (human scrutiny of accuracy, bias, reproducibility), and transparency (disclose tools, versions, parameters, and prompts in proposals, publications, and dissertations).
initiatives.significance: Gives UR researchers a clear, committee-endorsed standard aligned with HIPAA/FERPA/IP policy and with emerging federal sponsor expectations.

investment.funding_source: Office of the Vice President for Research
investment.description: Internal policy effort by AI in Research Committee.

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns research use of GenAI with HIPAA, FERPA, IP, and data-classification policies.

initiatives.title: BlueHive H100 GPU partition for CoE-DSAI affiliated PIs
initiatives.description: Through a strategic investment by the Center of Excellence in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence, Rochester added a dedicated BlueHive HPC partition with NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs for priority access by CoE-DSAI PIs, on top of existing K80/V100/A100 nodes and ~1,440 TFLOPS / 5.2 PB storage at the Center for Integrated Research Computing (CIRC).
initiatives.significance: Expands UR's internal GPU capacity for generative AI and foundation model work at a moment when most peer institutions are rate-limited by compute.

investment.funding_source: Center of Excellence in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (Empire State Development funding flow)
investment.description: Capital investment in H100 GPU nodes; specific dollar amount not disclosed.

tools_and_vendors.name: BlueHive HPC H100 partition
tools_and_vendors.role: Dedicated GPU partition for CoE-DSAI AI research

initiatives.title: MS in Healthcare Data Science and AI (online)
initiatives.description: Delivered jointly by the Goergen Institute for Data Science and AI and the UR Health Lab at URMC, the online MS targets working clinical and technical professionals. It sits alongside UR's MS in Data Science, Advanced Certificate in Data Science, and Simon Business School's online MS in Business Analytics and Applied AI.
initiatives.significance: Directly ties AI degree revenue to URMC's clinical AI strategy and to the regional health-and-education workforce pipeline.

investment.funding_source: Tuition-funded program; co-administered by Goergen Institute and UR Health Lab

collaborations.description: Cross-school joint program with the Medical Center.

initiatives.title: Goergen Institute renamed to include Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: In 2024 the Goergen Institute for Data Science was renamed the Goergen Institute for Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (GIDS-AI) to reflect its expanded AI portfolio. Mujdat Cetin (Robin and Tim Wentworth Director) was reappointed to a second five-year term leading 100+ affiliated faculty across the university.
initiatives.significance: Signals that AI is now a named, institution-level research priority rather than a subset of data science.

investment.funding_source: Hajim School of Engineering and Applied Sciences / Provost
investment.description: Renaming and reappointment; no separate budget disclosed.

initiatives.title: Marketing & Communications GenAI Guidelines
initiatives.description: University Marketing and Communications convened an internal GenAI committee and published guidelines governing GenAI use in web content, brand language, imagery, and external communications, complementing the Provost's educational guidance.
initiatives.significance: Extends UR's governance into brand, web, and public-facing content — a channel often under-governed at peer R1s.

investment.description: Internal policy committee; no dedicated budget disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Specifies when and how GenAI output may be used in official university content.

initiatives.title: Greene Center AI career channel
initiatives.description: Rochester's Gwen M. Greene Center for Career Education and Connections stood up a dedicated Artificial Intelligence career channel (careereducation.rochester.edu) that curates AI job markets, internships, skill pathways, and employer partners for undergraduate and graduate students.
initiatives.significance: Operationalizes workforce AI readiness at the student-services level rather than leaving it to individual academic programs.

investment.description: Internal operating investment; no dedicated dollar amount disclosed.

collaborations.description: Employer partners curated through the Greene Center AI channel.

The Laboratory for Laser Energetics operates the Conesus supercomputer for physics workloads. Empire AI consortium membership (Feb 2025) adds access to the $275M+ state AI computing center at University at Buffalo. Exact GPU counts are not publicly disclosed.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Equitable access principle in GenAI in Education Guidance
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: The Provost's guidance explicitly lists equitable access to GenAI tools and resources as a guiding principle, tying university-provided access (chat.rochester.edu) to the requirement that instructors not assume students can afford external paid tools.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: University accessibility services (applies to AI tools)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UR's accessibility office coordinates reasonable accommodations that now include disclosure expectations for AI tools used by students with disabilities.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Office of Equity & Inclusion Strategic Incentive Fund
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: University-wide fund supporting EDI efforts, including AI-adjacent faculty hiring and research.

regulatory_context.summary: New York State signed the Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act on December 19, 2025, creating an AI oversight office inside the Department of Financial Services and imposing transparency, safety-protocol publication, and 72-hour incident-reporting obligations on large frontier AI developers (with penalties up to $1M first / $3M subsequent). The Act primarily targets developers of very large models rather than universities directly, but it frames the state compliance environment. Earlier in 2024–2025 the state stood up Empire AI, a consortium UR joined in February 2025 with $275M+ in state funding. UR is a private university with no state-system AI policy above it, but it participates in state-funded consortia (Empire AI, CoE-DSAI, NYSTAR).

controversies_and_litigation.summary: Class action filed against the University of Rochester over the May 2023 MOVEit data breach (~88,000 individuals, SSNs exposed). Pre-dates UR's GenAI platform but shapes its data-security posture.
URMC settled a $2.85M class action over Facebook Pixel / patient data transmission practices — relevant context for URMC's emphasis on HIPAA-compliant AI tooling.

narrative_summary:
The University of Rochester is a mid-sized private R1 (~11,946 students) whose AI posture punches above its weight because it couples a conventional academic AI footprint with an unusually large integrated health system (URMC, eight hospitals, 32,000 employees) and a deep optics/physics computing base. Under President Sarah Mangelsdorf, Provost Nicole Sampson, and CIO Julie Myers, Rochester has built out a five-domain AI Council (Administrative, AI Tools, Education, Clinical, Research) with named co-leads rather than appointing a single Chief AI Officer. Policy is comprehensive and documented: the Provost's December 2024 GenAI in Education Guidance requires syllabus-level rules, the Faculty Senate AI in Research Committee issued parallel research guidance the same month, a March 2025 provostal directive bars sensitive data from public AI tools, and Marketing and URMC each added sub-committees.

The anchor deployment is chat.rochester.edu / UR AI Chat v2, a GPT-4o-on-Azure platform (launched January 2025, v2 May 2025) with a Document Agent and approval for moderate/high-risk data. At URMC, a parallel enterprise stack is emerging: Microsoft PowerScribe One with Smart Impression plus Dragon Copilot preview in radiology, and a 2025 enterprise-wide deployment of Qualified Health covering 32,000 employees. On the research side, Rochester renamed the Goergen Institute to include Artificial Intelligence (GIDS-AI) in 2024, upgraded CIRC's BlueHive cluster with NVIDIA H100 GPUs funded by CoE-DSAI, and joined New York's Empire AI consortium in February 2025 — one of only three new members. Christopher Kanan's AI Horizons Institute is UR's bid for a federally funded NSF AI Institute, backed by Mangelsdorf's $8.5M transdisciplinary-centers commitment.

Gaps: UR has no single Chief AI Officer, explicit dollar figures for most deployments are undisclosed, and Rochester acknowledges in its own planning materials that it has fewer than ten core AI faculty compared with 50+ at Cornell/Columbia/NYU — a scale deficit the AI Horizons proposal is meant to close.

gaps_acknowledged: No single Chief AI Officer is named; AI strategy leadership is distributed across AI Council domain co-leads.
Specific dollar amounts for the chat.rochester.edu license, the Qualified Health contract, and the BlueHive H100 investment are not publicly disclosed.
Exact GPU counts at CIRC/BlueHive are not publicly disclosed.
Rochester's internal planning materials note fewer than ten core AI faculty, but no public headcount is published.
The AI Horizons Institute NSF proposal decision has not been reported.
No documented controversy tied specifically to generative AI use (FERPA violations, AI-driven cheating cases, or faculty/student disputes) surfaced in this research cycle.
